examples.title: Nested Structure for Employee Management
examples.description: Complete program demonstrating nested structures for employee with personal details, address, and salary
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates nested structures with three levels: Employee contains Address and Salary as nested members. Initialization uses nested braces, and display shows accessing nested members via both dot and arrow operators.

examples.title: Accessing Nested Structure Members
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating member access through nested structures using dot and arrow operators
examples.code:
// PSEUDOCODE FOR NESTED STRUCTURE ACCESS

// Define inner structures
STRUCT Date
    day: INTEGER
    month: INTEGER
    year: INTEGER
END STRUCT

STRUCT Address
    houseNo: STRING
    city: STRING
    pincode: INTEGER
END STRUCT

// Define outer structure with nested structures
STRUCT Employee
    name: STRING
    empId: INTEGER
    birthDate: Date        // Nested structure
    joinDate: Date         // Nested structure
    residentialAddr: Address   // Nested structure
END STRUCT

// Main algorithm
ALGORITHM NestedAccess
BEGIN
    DECLARE emp: Employee
    
    // Initializing nested structure using dot operator
    emp.name ← "Alice"
    emp.empId ← 101
    emp.birthDate.day ← 15
    emp.birthDate.month ← 8
    emp.birthDate.year ← 1995
    
    // Accessing nested members
    PRINT emp.birthDate.year    // Direct access
    
    // Using pointer and arrow operator
    DECLARE ptr: Pointer to Employee
    ptr ← ADDRESS OF emp
    PRINT ptr->residentialAddr.city    // Arrow then dot
    
    // Nested initialization shortcut
    emp ← {"Bob", 102, {1, 1, 2000}, {1, 6, 2020}, {"123", "Mumbai", 400001}}
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the hierarchical nature of nested structures and demonstrates both dot (.) and arrow (->) operators for accessing deeply nested members. The initialization syntax uses nested braces corresponding to each structure level.

examples.title: Python Equivalent of Nested Structures
examples.description: Demonstrates how nested structures in C translate to Python classes with composition
examples.code:
# Python equivalent of nested structures in C

# Inner class for Date
class Date:
    def __init__(self, day=0, month=0, year=0):
        self.day = day
        self.month = month
        self.year = year

# Inner class for Address
class Address:
    def __init__(self, city="", state="", pincode=0):
        self.city = city
        self.state = state
        self.pincode = pincode

# Outer class using composition (like nested structure)
class Employee:
    def __init__(self, name="", emp_id=0, birth_date=None, address=None):
        self.name = name
        self.emp_id = emp_id
        self.birth_date = birth_date if birth_date else Date()
        self.address = address if address else Address()
    
    def display(self):
        print(f"Name: {self.name}")
        print(f"ID: {self.emp_id}")
        print(f"Birth Date: {self.birth_date.day}/{self.birth_date.month}/{self.birth_date.year}")
        print(f"City: {self.address.city}")

# Creating nested objects (equivalent to C nested structure)
emp = Employee(
    name="John",
    emp_id=101,
    birth_date=Date(15, 8, 1990),
    address=Address("Delhi", "Delhi", 110001)
)

# Accessing nested members (equivalent to emp.birth_date.day)
print(emp.birth_date.day)    # Output: 15
print(emp.address.city)      # Output: Delhi
emp.display()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates composition which is the object-oriented equivalent of C's nested structures. Instead of struct within struct, we use classes within classes, but the access pattern (object.nested_object.member) remains similar.

examples.title: Passing Nested Structures to Functions
examples.description: Demonstrates pass by value and pass by reference of nested structures
examples.explanation: This program shows three ways to work with nested structures and functions: (1) Pass by value creates a copy, (2) Pass by reference uses arrow operator to modify original, (3) Functions can return nested structures. The print statements show memory addresses to demonstrate the difference between pass by value and reference.

examples.title: Java Equivalent Using Composition
examples.description: Java program showing composition as equivalent to nested structures in C
examples.explanation: Java uses composition (has-a relationship) which is equivalent to nested structures in C. The main difference is that Java has encapsulation with private members and getters/setters, whereas C structures have public members by default. The access pattern employee.address.getCity() is similar to C's emp.address.city.
